title: PM context and design constraints
use_when: PM context and design constraints. Use when the user needs a product workflow for stakeholder management related to pm context and design constraints. Trigger terms: product-design, requirements, design-constraints, stakeholder-management.
do_not_use_when: Do not use PM context and design constraints when the request is mainly metric diagnosis, pricing, GTM, or implementation planning without UX evidence; route to the narrower Productize skill that owns that artifact instead. Do not use it when the user lacks enough context to distinguish facts, assumptions, and decision risk.
output_artifact: PM context and design constraints UX/design review with findings, constraints, fixes, and acceptance checks
framework_fit: PM context and design constraints fits Design work in the Design lifecycle when the user needs PM context and design constraints UX/design review with findings, constraints, fixes, and acceptance checks and has enough product context to make tradeoffs explicit. Use it to produce a decision-ready artifact, not a framework tour.
failure_modes: Produces PM context and design constraints UX/design review with findings, constraints, fixes, and acceptance checks without tying recommendations to the user's Design stage, evidence, and decision pressure.
Treats PM context and design constraints as generic Design advice instead of naming assumptions, missing inputs, risks, and the next validation step.
Fails to route away from PM context and design constraints when another Productize skill owns the requested artifact more directly.

examples.prompt: Use $pm-context-and-design-constraints to turn this context context into a decision-ready PM context and design constraints UX/design review with findings, constraints, fixes, and acceptance checks.
examples.expected_artifact: PM context and design constraints UX/design review with findings, constraints, fixes, and acceptance checks
